perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS A. NLS carries on the business of researching, developing and manufacturing certain pharmaceutical products and is the legal and beneficial owner of certain Intellectual Property, including Patents, Know-how and other materials (all words with capital letters are defined below);
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS A. NLS carries on the business of researching, developing and manufacturing certain pharmaceutical products and is the legal and beneficial owner of certain Intellectual Property, including Patents and other materials (all words with capital letters are defined below);
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Know-how' from the definition of Intellectual Property owned by NLS, it creates an in-text contradiction because subsequent sections rely on Know-how being part of the Intellectual Property licensed to Eurofarma. This omission causes uncertainty in the enforcement of clauses related to the transfer and protection of Know-how, particularly Article 2.1(iii) and Article 10.2, because it's no longer clearly defined as Intellectual Property within the initial clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  Grant of License 2.1. Grant of rights Subject to the conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts, (i) an exclusive, fee-bearing, non-transferable distribution right of the Licensed Product in the Territory during the Term (the "Distribution"), (ii) an exclusive, royalty-free, non-transferable license to the Licensor's Patents and Trademarks to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term, without the right to sublicense (the "License"), and (iii) an exclusive, royalty-bearing, non-transferable license to the Intellectual Property (except Patents and Trademarks ) and the Developed Intellectual Property and a right to obtain the technology transfer of the Know-How to manufacture the Licensed Product in the Territory during the Term, pursuant to the conditions set forth in Article 2.3 below and elsewhere in this Agreement (the "Technology Transfer"), all to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, manufacture, have manufactured, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term, without the right to sublicense.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'Know-how' within the definition of Intellectual Property. The changed text omits 'Know-how', thereby narrowing the definition of Intellectual Property. Article 2.1(iii) in the contradicted text grants a license to 'Intellectual Property' and specifically mentions 'Know-How'. Because the modified text removes Know-how from the initial definition of Intellectual Property, it contradicts Article 2.1(iii) by creating uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding whether the license granted in Article 2.1(iii) still includes Know-how, as Know-how is no longer clearly defined as Intellectual Property.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3. Technology Transfer After Licensor and Licensee have agreed to move forward to the Technology Transfer phase, the Parties will execute a separate Technology Transfer Agreement, which shall be at arms-length basis and pursuant to the conditions herein. For that purpose, Licensor hereby undertakes to transfer to Licensee all Know-how in order to enable Licensee to implement it in its plant to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, manufacture, have manufactured, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term. Licensee undertake to use the transferred Know-how solely for the purposes and limits provided in this Agreement. For the avoidance of any doubt, the Know-how does not include Licensee Know-how Licensor shall render to Licensee all technical assistance necessary to enable Licensee to fully implement the Technology Transfer phase in Licensee's premises. The royalties provided in this Agreement already includes such technical assistance, unless the Parties otherwise mutually and in good-faith agree. In such case, the Parties will provide in separate agreement the additional conditions for the Technology Transfer, which shall be at arms-length basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3. Technology Transfer After Licensor and Licensee have agreed to move forward to the Technology Transfer phase, the Parties will execute a separate Technology Transfer Agreement, which shall be at arms-length basis and pursuant to the conditions herein. For that purpose, Licensor hereby undertakes to provide Licensee all information necessary for successful technology transfer to enable Licensee to implement it in its plant to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, manufacture, have manufactured, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term. Licensee undertake to use the transferred information solely for the purposes and limits provided in this Agreement. For the avoidance of any doubt, such information does not include Licensee Know-how Licensor shall render to Licensee all technical assistance necessary to enable Licensee to fully implement the Technology Transfer phase in Licensee's premises. The royalties provided in this Agreement already includes such technical assistance, unless the Parties otherwise mutually and in good-faith agree. In such case, the Parties will provide in separate agreement the additional conditions for the Technology Transfer, which shall be at arms-length basis.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Know-how' to 'all information necessary for successful technology transfer' in Article 2.3 introduces an in-text contradiction. While the original text specifically defines the transfer of 'Know-how,' this change broadens the scope to any necessary information without defining what that entails. This creates ambiguity, particularly in relation to the original definition of 'Know-how' in Section 1, because the specific protections and obligations tied to Know-how are now diluted. Subsequent references to Know-how, such as in Article 10.2 concerning the transfer of Know-how, become unclear as the exact nature of what is being transferred is no longer consistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2. Transfer of Know-how Each Party shall, at the other Party's request, transfer to the other Party any and all Know-how relating to each Licensed Product that the other Party reasonably needs in order to perform its obligations or exploit its rights under this Agreement. Each Party shall use such Know-how solely for the purpose of performing its obligations or exploiting its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Know-how' to 'all information necessary for successful technology transfer' introduces a significant contradiction with Section 10.2. Originally, Section 10.2 concerns the transfer of 'Know-how' as a defined term. By broadening the scope to include any and all 'information necessary', the specific obligations and protections associated with 'Know-how' are now ambiguous. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear if Section 10.2's requirement to transfer 'Know-how' is satisfied by transferring 'information'. The change dilutes the term and therefore breaks consistency with Section 10.2.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3. Royalties For the Technology Transfer and as applicable for the License granted by NLS to Eurofarma under article 2.1(iii) hereabove, Eurofarma shall pay to NLS royalty payments (the "Royalties") on the annual Net Sales in the Territory according to the following table: Annual Net Sales in the Territory in USD: Royalty in Percent of Net Sales under 10 million 7% 10 million to < 20 million 8% 20 million to < 30 million 9% 30 million and above 10% Royalty payments shall be paid quarterly within thirty (45) days following the close of the calendar quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3. Royalties Eurofarma shall pay to NLS royalty payments (the "Royalties") on the annual Net Sales in the Territory according to the following table: Annual Net Sales in the Territory in USD: Royalty in Percent of Net Sales under 10 million 7% 10 million to < 20 million 8% 20 million to < 30 million 9% 30 million and above 10% Royalty payments shall be paid quarterly within thirty (45) days following the close of the calendar quarter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "For the Technology Transfer and as applicable for the License granted by NLS to Eurofarma under article 2.1(iii) hereabove", from the beginning of Section 3.3., the basis for the royalty payments becomes ambiguous. Without this explicit connection to Technology Transfer, Section 3.3, contradicts section 2.1, where it is stated that royalties will be paid for Intellectual Property, Now the section lacks the reference on where this royalty applies. This omission introduces uncertainty about which activities or rights are subject to the royalty, potentially leading to disputes over payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  Grant of License 2.1. Grant of rights Subject to the conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts, (i) an exclusive, fee-bearing, non-transferable distribution right of the Licensed Product in the Territory during the Term (the "Distribution"), (ii) an exclusive, royalty-free, non-transferable license to the Licensor's Patents and Trademarks to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term, without the right to sublicense (the "License"), and (iii) an exclusive, royalty-bearing, non-transferable license to the Intellectual Property (except Patents and Trademarks ) and the Developed Intellectual Property and a right to obtain the technology transfer of the Know-How to manufacture the Licensed Product in the Territory during the Term, pursuant to the conditions set forth in Article 2.3 below and elsewhere in this Agreement (the "Technology Transfer"), all to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, have commercialized, promote, have promoted, distribute, have distributed, manufacture, have manufactured, sell and have sold the Licensed Product during the Term, without the right to sublicense.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.3 explicitly linked the royalty payments to 'Technology Transfer and as applicable for the License granted by NLS to Eurofarma under article 2.1(iii)'. By removing this phrase, the modified Section 3.3 creates ambiguity regarding the basis for royalty payments. This directly contradicts Section 2.1, which specifies royalty-bearing licenses for Intellectual Property and Technology Transfer under Article 2.1(iii). The omission now casts doubt on whether the royalty payments apply to Intellectual Property or the Technology Transfer, leading to a clear contradiction and potential dispute over payment obligations. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition provided.
